avoid_months: third week of September onward (season wound up)
snow_or_rain_notes: Snow present early June to mid-July
Snow starts melting in later part of July
No snow in autumn months on Pin Bhaba Pass
First snowfall of the season towards end of September blocks access to Spiti
Most of the trek lies in the rain shadow area of the Himalayas (Pin valley of Spiti region)

terrain_challenges: River crossings with strong currents (Bhaba tributary between Yangpa and Mulling, Pin River after the pass)
Extensive scree zones before and after Pin Bhaba Pass
Moraine and loose scree on Pass Crossing Day
Rockfall zones on Pass Crossing Day
altitude_or_weather_risks: Pin Bhaba Pass at 16,105 ft above sea level
Camping at Phutsirang over 13,000 ft before the pass crossing
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) even with an extra acclimatisation day
High-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers carried by Indiahikes team
Rainfall or snowfall lasting hours can change trek dynamics

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 10 km in under 60 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date
Without approval you will not be allowed on the trek
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the fitness criteria
Record runs using apps like Nike or Strava
Paste your public fitness profile link on your Indiahikes account
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
6-week plan provided for those not currently active

experience_themes: Monsoon months are the best time to do the trek
Lush green meadows with wildflowers blooming, especially at Karah
Transition zone at Phutsirang with colourful moss-like vegetation on soil/rocks
Stark contrast at the pass between green Kinnaur side and pink Spiti side
primary_watchouts: High snow near the pass in June makes it risky (not impossible)
Trail gets slightly more comfortable from July onward
Landslides and roadblocks more frequent during monsoon, can cause traffic jams and delays en route to base camp
First snowfall towards end of September blocks access to Spiti region
Indiahikes winds up trekking season from third week of September
Trekkers averse to monsoon-related road delays and landslides
best_for: Trekkers wanting lush green meadows and wildflowers (July-August)
Trekkers wanting clear skies and autumn colours (mid-September)
Trekkers wanting dramatic contrast between Kinnaur greens and Spiti pinks at the pass
not_ideal_for: Trekkers averse to monsoon-related road delays and landslides
Trekkers planning tight itineraries without buffer days
Trekkers wanting to avoid snow risk near the pass (June)
Trekkers not prepared for long days of more than 10 km at high altitude
